commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]

target.title: Fix getSubagentDepth for legacy subagent session keys (AI-assisted)
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `getSubagentDepth()` returned `0` for legacy subagent session keys like `subagent:worker` because it only counted occurrences of the substring `":subagent:"`.
- Why it matters: Call sites that use depth as a fallback signal can misclassify subagent sessions as non-subagent when session-store metadata is missing, impacting announce/delivery and depth gating.
- What changed: `getSubagentDepth()` now counts `subagent:` segments at token boundaries (`(^|:)subagent:`) within the agent-scoped portion of the key.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No changes to session key shapes, parsing rules, or any subagent spawn/store metadata.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable)

- Root cause: `isSubagentSessionKey()` treated `subagent:<id>` as a valid subagent session key, but `getSubagentDepth()` only counted `":subagent:"` (missing the legacy prefix form).
- Missing detection / guardrail: No unit coverage for legacy `subagent:<id>` depth.
- Prior context (`git blame`, prior PR, issue, or refactor if known): Unknown.
- Why this regressed now: Unknown (likely legacy compatibility gap).
- If unknown, what was ruled out: N/A.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/routing/session-key.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in:
  - `getSubagentDepth("subagent:worker") === 1`
  - nested legacy key `subagent:parent:subagent:child` depth counts both segments
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: It directly asserts the depth behavior for the legacy key format without requiring session store state.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): A canonical nested agent-scoped case existed; it did not cover the legacy prefix format.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

None.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (Yes/No): No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (Yes/No): No
- New/changed network calls? (Yes/No): No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (Yes/No): No
- Data access scope changed? (Yes/No): No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: local dev
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A

### Steps

1. Call `getSubagentDepth("subagent:worker")`.

### Expected

- Returns `1`.

### Actual

- Returned `0` prior to this change.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios:
  - Unit tests: `pnpm test -- src/routing/session-key.test.ts`
  - Typecheck: `pnpm exec tsgo -p tsconfig.json --noEmit`
  - Formatting: `pnpm format:check`
- Edge cases checked:
  - Nested legacy `subagent:parent:subagent:child`
  - Canonical nested `agent:main:subagent:parent:subagent:child`
- What you did **not** verify:
  - Full gateway runtime / channel integrations

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (Yes/No): Yes
- Config/env changes? (Yes/No): No
- Migration needed? (Yes/No): No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: Revert this commit.
- Files/config to restore: N/A
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for:
  - Incorrect depth calculations for subagent session keys

## Risks and Mitigations

None.

---

### AI-assisted disclosure

This PR was AI-assisted (OpenClaw subagent + LLM). I reviewed the diff and verified behavior via the listed unit tests/typecheck/format check.
